summary: Parse optional `format` (closed vocab, first entry yaml-frontmatter) + a unit-shape key on a kind definition into typed inert CustomKind fields the adapter will later consume

per.section: Decision: the adapter faces are declared — a kind names its projection format

edit.description: Teach `CustomKind::from_header` two optional header keys the Decision names, without any adapter consuming them yet: `format` — a closed-vocabulary projection format whose only harvested value is `yaml-frontmatter` (any other value is a load error, mirroring the closed extraction-primitive guard); and a unit-shape key — a closed enum distinguishing a lone file (id from the stem) from a directory-with-companions (id from the dir name). Add BOTH to the `matches!` allowed-key guard (kind.rs:110-118) so they parse instead of tripping `KindError::UnknownKey`, and store them as typed `Option` fields on the `CustomKind` struct (kind.rs:60-74) — absent ⇒ `None`, still valid, since today's built-in KIND.md carry neither. Add a `KindError` variant for an out-of-vocab `format`/unit-shape value. The fields are inert: nothing reads them until DECLARED-FRONTMATTER-ADAPTER. Extend the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (kind.rs:815+) with coverage per the tests entry.

tests.asserts: in the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: absent `format`/unit-shape keys parse (the built-in-kind default, still valid); `format = "yaml-frontmatter"` and each unit-shape variant parse into the typed fields; an unknown `format` value and an unknown unit-shape value are load errors; and a KIND.md header carrying both keys no longer trips `UnknownKey`.

acceptance: `CustomKind` carries typed optional `format` + unit-shape fields; `from_header` accepts both keys on built-in and project KIND.md, rejects out-of-vocab values, and treats absence as valid; nothing consumes the fields yet; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Inbox sequencing fix: `from_header` (kind.rs:111) rejects unknown keys, and built-in KIND.md parses through that same guard (builtin_kind.rs:81) — a human adding format/unit lines to kinds/{skill,rule}/KIND.md before the parser accepts them turns every check red. Ships FIRST; then the human adds the two curated lines (chore); then DECLARED-FRONTMATTER-ADAPTER un-parks, main green at every step.

summary: Collapse the per-kind adapter modules (src/skill.rs, src/rule.rs) into one generic declaration-driven frontmatter adapter for import/apply/re-add/drift; retire the typed IRs

per.section: Decision: the adapter faces are declared — a kind names its projection format

gate.reason: the adapter is declaration-driven, but it needs the declared `format`/unit-shape fields parsed onto CustomKind first (FORMAT-KEY-PARSE, filed above), and kinds/skill+rule/KIND.md must then carry the format+unit lines — a human chore, since build's fence excludes kinds/ (curated std-lib territory, .flume/chain.ts). Un-parks once FORMAT-KEY-PARSE ships and the two curated KIND.md lines are added. Its ADAPTER-EQUIVALENCE-PIN prerequisite shipped (4765d5b).

new.description: The one generic frontmatter adapter the Decision names — implements the `yaml-frontmatter` projection format once, for every kind that declares it. Import: split the external artifact per the kind's declared unit shape (lone file, id from stem; directory with companions, id from dir name) and lift the kind's declared extraction `field`s into `[clause.<field>]` header tables (over the existing `Document` add_clause/add_satisfies/add_edge machinery). Apply: render the member document back to the harness format byte-deterministically. Drift: compare declared fields with no per-kind serializer. Re-add: re-parse an on-disk edit into the member document. Replaces Skill/Rule::to_document, skill_fields/rule_fields, and the per-kind import/re-add writers.

edit.description: Consume the declared projection facts FORMAT-KEY-PARSE parsed onto `CustomKind` (the `format` + unit-shape `Option` fields): drive the generic adapter's unit split and byte-render off them. FORMAT-KEY-PARSE owns the struct fields, the header parse, and the closed allowed-key guard (kind.rs:110-118); this entry only reads them (add accessor helpers if needed), never re-parses or re-guards.
Add `pub mod frontmatter;`; remove `pub mod skill;` and `pub mod rule;` (this entry owns these lines).

Replace `import_skill`/`import_rule` and their `existing_surface_*` mirrors with the generic frontmatter adapter driven by the kind definition; generalize `run`'s hardcoded skill/rule loop and the bare-root-skill special case; drop `use crate::skill`/`crate::rule` and the `ImportError::Skill`/`Rule` variants.

Replace `skill_fields`/`rule_fields` with the generic serializer; generalize `diff`/`apply`/`re_add`/`load_last_applied`/`update_lock` off the hardwired ["skill","rule"] lists to the registered kind set; drop `use crate::skill`/`crate::rule`.

Make `Workspace` kind-generic (a member list over `Unit`/the generic member type) instead of `Vec<Skill>`/`Vec<RuleArtifact>`; drop the `Skill`/`Rule` imports and the per-kind `Workspace::load` branches. The check gate is already generic; this is the adapter-face `Workspace` the drift/bundle/read faces consume.
Adapt bundle's `Workspace::load` consumption and its `Skill::from_source_dir` test helper to the generic member surface.
Adapt the read family (`why`/`requirements`) narration, which loads the typed `Workspace`, to the generic member surface; features already flow through the generic path unchanged.
Remove the `#[cfg(test)]` `skill_surface_unit`/`rule_surface_unit` parity helpers that build a `Unit` from a `Skill`/`Rule` (the IR↔generic parity they pinned is subsumed once the IR is gone).

tests.asserts: the generic adapter reproduces the pinned baseline exactly — import→apply byte-identical, YAML ordering stable, `extra` preserved, no-frontmatter rule, companions copied — for both skill and rule with zero per-kind engine code.

acceptance: src/skill.rs and src/rule.rs are deleted; one generic frontmatter adapter reads each kind's declared format + unit shape and drives import/apply/re-add/drift for skill and rule identically; ADAPTER-EQUIVALENCE-PIN's snapshots are unchanged; the built-in/custom split is purely source; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Now gated on TWO steps: (1) FORMAT-KEY-PARSE ships the inert format/unit-shape parse (else curated KIND.md lines redden main); (2) humans add `format="yaml-frontmatter"` + a unit shape to kinds/{skill,rule}/KIND.md (directory+companions for skill, flat file for rule; build's fence excludes kinds/). Then it un-parks. ADAPTER-EQUIVALENCE-PIN shipped. Atomic: add adapter, rewire import/drift/check/bundle/main, delete skill.rs+rule.rs in one green commit.

summary: Extend the extraction algebra with the two primitives 15-kinds.md lists but code lacks — a `fenced` block extractor and key-path (nested) `field` lookup — held until a consumer kind needs them

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the extraction vocabulary is harvested from the built-in kinds and extended deliberately (15-kinds.md Decision: extraction is a closed algebra); no current kind (skill/rule/spec) reads a fenced block or a nested key-path, so adding either now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it. Revive when a kind demands it — MEMORY-KIND's `@path` executed-reference syntax, a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field, or a spec class kind's fenced-block extraction.

edit.description: Two closed-vocabulary additions to `Primitive` (`specs/15-kinds.md`): (1) a `Fenced` primitive extracting fenced code-block content — the markdown-structure extractor the spec lists (`a fenced block`) but the enum omits (today: field/headings/sections/line_count/placement, kind.rs:220-241); (2) make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises — the impl currently does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` (kind.rs:263) so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. Both extend the closed-vocab load-guard's accepted primitive set. This entry owns both edits so they don't collide in parallel worktrees.

The extractor helpers behind the two primitives: a `body_fenced_blocks`-style reader over the markdown body (mirroring `body_headings`/`body_sections`), and key-path traversal for the field extractor (walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice, `specs/10-contracts.md`).

tests.asserts: a `fenced` primitive yields the fenced block's content and skips prose; a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; both preserve the source scalar kind.

acceptance: `Primitive::Fenced` and a nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` are in the closed vocabulary, load-guarded, and extract over a `Unit`; a KIND.md declaring either reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes one.)
notes: Disk audit re-confirmed both gaps this tick: `Primitive` = field/headings/sections/line_count/placement (kind.rs:220-241); `Field` reads flat `frontmatter.get(key)` (kind.rs:263), no key-path. Neither breaks anything today (no kind uses them). ONE entry — both touch src/kind.rs+src/extract.rs. On revival, serialize the shared-file edits against any open entry (esp. FORMAT-KEY-PARSE / DECLARED-FRONTMATTER-ADAPTER, which also edit src/kind.rs).

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), beside kinds/skill and kinds/rule: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared projection format + unit shape (post DECLARED-FRONTMATTER-ADAPTER), composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list (kind.rs:30) so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const and register it in the built-in package map beside `skill.anthropic`/`rule.anthropic`. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Rewritten for the declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs typed IR — the generic adapter projects it. The agent kind is a curated kinds/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md (outside build's fence) plus near-zero engine wiring: add "agent" to BUILTIN_KINDS (kind.rs) + an AGENT_PACKAGE binding (builtin.rs); build.rs auto-embeds. Deferred: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under requirements/satisfies, and blocked on the adapter wave draining.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md — the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs — no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob — so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/55's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/55). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both are root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence — parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
